title: When Assessing Morality: - Focus on Intent: Ask,
subtitle: Judge Actions, Not Outcomes
description:
When assessing morality:
- Focus on intent: Ask, "What was their intention, regardless of the outcome?"
- Separate luck from action: Consider how chance influenced the result.
- Be fair: Avoid overpraising or blaming based on outcomes alone.

Example: If someone saves a life by chance, their intent to help matters as much as the lucky outcome.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Log five recent evaluations to establish a baseline for intention-aware judgments. Note the outcomes and initial reactions to understand current biases.

howTo.title: Run the 3-day experiment
howTo.summary: For three consecutive days, apply the micro-script to at least three events daily. Document the outcomes, inferred intentions, and luck estimates.

howTo.title: Reflect on changes
howTo.summary: After the experiment, compare your findings to the baseline. Assess how focusing on intention has altered your judgments and feedback.

howTo.title: Share insights with your team
howTo.summary: Discuss the results of your experiment with your team. Encourage them to adopt the micro-script and emphasize the importance of intention in evaluations.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit of assessing intent. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/judge-intent-not-outcomes/.
